description: Full prediction lifecycle log. Every prediction lives here from creation to resolution. Day-by-day log entries are added during each briefing session. RCA is written on WRONG outcomes. Framework updates are proposed here and promoted to signal-framework.json on approval.

predictions.prediction: SWIGGY will test or breach the ₹260 52-week low within 7 days given no positive catalyst and continued broader market weakness
predictions.reasoning: SWIGGY sits 3.2% above its 52wk low of ₹266.30. Nifty fell 2.14% on March 30. FII selling continues. No earnings catalyst until Q4 results. At ₹260 stop-loss clusters will trigger forced selling. Short timeframe validates/invalidates the technical setup quickly.
predictions.outcome_evidence: On April 8, SWIGGY closed at ₹281.45 (+4.51% vs April 7). Market rallied on ceasefire news. SWIGGY failed to breach ₹260 target. Instead, the stock recovered as Brent crude crashed and broader market sentiment turned positive.

postmortem.root_cause: Regime change from DEADLINE_EVE_BINARY to DE-ESCALATION_REAL was NOT anticipated
postmortem.missed_factor: Ceasefire agreement was not incorporated into the bearish case. The prediction was built on the assumption of either strikes or extended war premium. A 2-week ceasefire + Hormuz protocol agreement was an unexpected positive catalyst.
postmortem.signal_missed: Geopolitical binary resolution typically creates regime shifts that override technical setup. The broader market relief on ceasefire overwhelmed stop-loss selling pressure.
postmortem.proposed_factor: M012_GEOPOLITICAL_RESOLUTION_RISK — when a major deadline has THREE possible outcomes (strikes/extension/deal), range the probability-weighted outcome. This prediction underweighted the 'deal' scenario.

predictions.prediction: Nifty will attempt to hold above 22,000 this week — DII buying will provide support even as FIIs continue selling
predictions.reasoning: Nifty at 22,331 after -2.14% on March 30 (FY26 close). The 22,000 level is a strong psychological support. DIIs have been net buyers offsetting FII selling. A weekly close below 22,000 would be technically significant. This tests the DII-vs-FII battle at a key level.
predictions.outcome_evidence: Nifty closed at 23,123.65 on April 7 (check_date), well above the 22,000 target. DII buying absorbed FII selling as predicted. Nifty held 22,000 throughout the week.

predictions.prediction: ASTRA MICRO will see profit-booking pullback to ₹950-980 range this week as defence sector sees rotation after the run
predictions.reasoning: ASTRA MICRO at ₹1,030 (+83% vs avg). Defence sector PE at 50.6x vs 10yr avg 36.6x. Broad market in selloff. When Nifty falls, high-PE high-gain stocks see disproportionate selling as investors book profits. Short-term momentum reversal likely.
predictions.outcome_evidence: On April 8, ASTRA MICRO closed at ₹1,019.45 (+2.31% vs April 7). Stock BREACHED ₹1,000 resistance and hit new highs. No pullback to ₹950-980 occurred. Instead, war premium extended as ceasefire is 2-week duration (best outcome for defense electronics).

postmortem.root_cause: Regime change: Trump's 2-week ceasefire + Hormuz protocol agreement was perceived as stabilizing the geopolitical situation SHORT-TERM. This extended war premium instead of unwinding it.
postmortem.missed_factor: The ceasefire is a 2-week truce, not a permanent peace. Markets are treating it as 'stable with ongoing negotiations', which EXTENDS war premium demand for defense stocks rather than unwinding it.
postmortem.signal_missed: Defense electronics thrive on 'extended tension' scenarios more than 'immediate escalation' or 'instant deal' scenarios. The 2-week ceasefire = best outcome for defense stocks (premium lasts longer).
postmortem.proposed_factor: M012_DEFENSE_SECTOR_DURATION_PREMIUM — War premium benefit is MAXIMIZED in 'extended truce' scenarios (2-6 weeks), not in 'strikes' (acute but short) or 'deal' (unwound immediately) scenarios.

predictions.prediction: SWIGGY will breach ₹260 (near 52-week low area) within 4 weeks if no positive earnings catalyst emerges
predictions.reasoning: At ₹268.55, just 3.2% above 52wk low of ₹266.30. No profitability catalyst visible. Quick-commerce war ongoing. 382 combined shares at significant loss.
predictions.confidence_note: WEAKENED: Stock at ₹281.45 on April 8 (ceasefire relief). Needs to fall -7.7% from current levels to breach ₹260. 17 days remaining. Ceasefire sentiment is temporary headwind to bearish thesis. Confidence reduced from 4 to 2.

predictions.prediction: ASTRA MICRO will face profit-booking correction to ₹850–₹900 range within 4 weeks as defence sector PE remains stretched
predictions.reasoning: At ₹1,030, +83% gain. Nifty Defence at 50.6x TTM PE. Broad market selling pressure. Profit-booking likely at these levels.
predictions.confidence_note: THESIS INVALIDATED: Ceasefire extended war premium. ASTRA MICRO at ₹1,019.45 is near new highs — requires -15-17% fall to reach ₹850-900 in 17 days. Defense sector is benefitting from 2-week managed tension scenario (M012). Confidence downgraded to 1. Will score WRONG at check_date.

predictions.prediction: IRFC will remain range-bound ₹85–₹95 for the next 4 weeks with no re-rating catalyst until Q4FY26 results
predictions.reasoning: IRFC depends on government capex allocation which is squeezed by the oil shock fiscal deficit. No near-term order flow or earnings surprise expected. The range prediction tests whether 'hold and wait' is the right call or if further downside emerges.
predictions.confidence_note: INTACT: Ceasefire positive for capex sentiment but IRFC is still a slow-moving infrastructure stock. Range thesis holds. 21 days remaining.

predictions.prediction: MRPL will fall to ₹160–₹170 range within 2 months if Brent crude stays above $100/bbl
predictions.reasoning: MRPL at ₹181.65 already down -7.5%. Crude at $104–$122 = GRM compression. Government blocking pass-through. Q4 results will show damage.
predictions.confidence_note: THESIS INVALIDATED: Brent crashed to $93.13 on April 8 (BELOW $95 invalidation threshold set at prediction creation). Thesis premise (Brent >$100) is now FALSE. Confidence downgraded to 1. Expect WRONG at check_date unless Brent recovers above $100 and sustains. P017 (bullish MRPL) is the replacement thesis.

predictions.prediction: Nifty will test the 22,000–22,200 range within 4–6 weeks given continued FII outflows and PMI weakness
predictions.reasoning: FIIs -₹1.04L cr CY2026, PMI at 3.5yr low, Nifty at 22,819. No near-term FII reversal catalyst. DII support insufficient to offset.
predictions.confidence_note: THESIS INVALIDATED: Nifty gapped to 23,997.35 on April 8 — 1,800 pts ABOVE the 22,000-22,200 target zone. Would require -9% fall from current levels to reach target. Confidence downgraded to 1. This is expected WRONG at check_date unless geopolitical risk returns sharply.

predictions.prediction: COAL INDIA will hold above ₹430 for the next 2 months as crude-coal substitution demand continues
predictions.reasoning: Coal India at ₹462. Crude at $104–$122 makes coal a cheaper industrial fuel alternative. JM Financial included in buy basket. Support at ₹430.
predictions.confidence_note: THESIS STRENGTHENED: Coal India at ~₹449.20 on April 8. Ceasefire + Brent crash to $93.13 further increases coal's cost advantage vs crude. ₹19 buffer above ₹430 target. 50 days remaining, confidence raised to 4.

predictions.prediction: INDIGO will underperform Nifty by 8–15% over 2 months as ATF cost spike hits Q4FY26 and Q1FY27 margins
predictions.reasoning: ATF = 35–40% of operating cost. Brent at $104–$122, up 50–75% from $70. Government ATF excise reform is separate from petrol/diesel cuts. Q4 margins will show the damage when results are announced.
predictions.confidence_note: THESIS WEAKENED: IndiGo at ₹4,614.90 on April 8 (+7.5% from entry ₹4,294.7). Nifty also up +5.1% from entry. P6 requires INDIGO to underperform by 8-15% = INDIGO must fall -3-10% relative to Nifty over remaining 52 days. Q4FY26 results (May) will show ATF margin damage — that remains the catalyst. But ceasefire has reduced the immediate pressure. Confidence maintained at 2. May resolve WRONG if Q4 results show less damage than feared.

predictions.prediction: NHPC will begin recovery toward ₹85–₹90 range within 2 months once FY27 budget capex visibility improves
predictions.reasoning: NHPC at -26.3% vs avg. The NHPC-ACME BESS deal (275MW/550MWh, Feb 2026) is a structural positive. Recovery depends on FY27 government capex commitment being maintained. If Q1FY27 spending data shows recovery, NHPC re-rates. Low confidence because timing is uncertain.
predictions.confidence_note: NEUTRAL: Ceasefire positive for PSU capex sentiment long-term. NHPC benefits from reduced fiscal pressure if crude prices stay down. Still early — 54 days remaining.

predictions.prediction: ASTRA MICRO will breach ₹1,000 and test ₹1,050–₹1,080 within 7 days if war premium sustains post-deadline
predictions.reasoning: ASTRA MICRO at ₹997, +77% from cost, approaching ₹1,000 psychological level. If Trump deadline results in strikes or extended war premium, defense electronics stocks benefit directly. Stock already showing +5.61% momentum today. However if Iran deal materializes, defense premium unwinds — hence confidence 3.
predictions.outcome_evidence: April 14 holiday. Last close (April 13): ₹1,019.25. Stock peaked at ₹1,132 during prediction window (exceeded target 1050-1080) but profit-booking on Iran outreach signal pushed it below target range at check date.
predictions.confidence_note: ON TRACK: ASTRA MICRO at ₹1,019.45 — already breached ₹1,000. Now needs +3-6% more to hit ₹1,050-1,080 target. April 10 Islamabad talks are key risk. 2-week ceasefire = managed tension = defense premium extended. Confidence raised to 4.

predictions.prediction: INDIGO will fall below ₹4,100 within 7 days as ATF costs and war premium weigh, regardless of Hormuz outcome
predictions.reasoning: INDIGO already down -1.01% today to ~₹4,251. ATF is 35-40% of operating costs. Brent at $110.15 — even if Hormuz deal reduces crude, current ATF costs are locking in margin compression for Q4FY26 results.
predictions.outcome_evidence: April 14 holiday. Last close (April 13): ₹4,427.20. IndiGo never came close to ₹4,100 target. Despite crude spike, airline demand resilience held stock well above target.
predictions.confidence_note: THESIS INVALIDATED: IndiGo closed at ₹4,614.90 on April 8 — UP 8.11% from April 7. Stock is 12.6% ABOVE the ₹4,100 target. Ceasefire + Brent crash to $93 completely reversed the ATF cost thesis. Requires -11.2% fall by April 14 which is very unlikely. Will score WRONG at check_date April 14. Confidence downgraded to 1.

predictions.prediction: Nifty IT index will outperform Nifty 50 by 5–10% over the next 4 weeks as USD strength + US Q1 earnings season drives global tech rotation into Indian IT
predictions.reasoning: April 7 session revealed IT-USD decoupling pattern (PAT002): HCLTECH +2.73%, INFY +2.54%, TCS +2.66% led market recovery despite war premium. USD strengthening makes Indian IT exporters more competitive. US Q1 earnings season (April-May) typically positive for IT sentiment. Even in Iran escalation scenario, IT stocks provide portfolio insulation as USD appreciates. Multi-factor thesis: USD strength + earnings season + defensive quality.
predictions.confidence_note: INTACT: TCS Q4 results on April 9. IT sector decoupling confirmed on April 7+8. TCS at ₹2,555.30. Thesis has 27 days remaining. If TCS Q4 results beat expectations, confidence raises to 4.

predictions.prediction: INDIGO will stabilize above ₹4,150 over next 7 days as ceasefire relief reduces ATF cost panic, but stock remains pressured by Q4FY26 margin headwinds
predictions.reasoning: Ceasefire removes acute escalation risk to ATF costs, but Q4FY26 results will show margin compression from peak Brent levels (avg $110+ in Q4). INDIGO stabilizes on relief but faces earnings headwind.
predictions.outcome_evidence: INDIGO closed ₹4,638.00 on April 15, well above the ₹4,150 stabilization target. Stock held above ₹4,150 throughout the 7-day window (April 8-15). The relief rally scenario materialized fully — ATF cost relief on declining Brent + Iran diplomacy. Target range of ₹4,150-4,300 was massively conservative — INDIGO surged to ₹4,614+ on April 8 itself. Core directional thesis CORRECT: stock stabilized and continued higher, not lower.
predictions.confidence_note: TARGET EXCEEDED ON ENTRY DAY: IndiGo closed at ₹4,614.90 on April 8, far above the ₹4,150-4,300 target range. This suggests target was too conservative. Will score as RIGHT (stable above ₹4,150) but the range was blown out by the ATF relief rally. Confidence raised to 4. Updating target band upward mentally to ₹4,500-4,700 to track consolidation.

predictions.prediction: Nifty will consolidate in 23900-24100 range over next 7 days as ceasefire relief rally momentum moderates and profit-booking sets in
predictions.reasoning: April 8 saw +3.78% gap-up on ceasefire announcement. This is a relief rally, not start of 5% trend. Nifty faces profit-booking, April 10 Islamabad talks, and earnings season. Consolidation reflects market pricing in the deal.
predictions.outcome_evidence: Nifty closed at 24,231.30 on April 15. Prediction was "consolidate in 23,900-24,100 range." The 23,900 floor held perfectly — Nifty never breached that level. But the 24,100 ceiling was broken significantly, closing 131 pts above. Iran-US talks imminent drove a relief rally that exceeded the consolidation band. Direction (no major selloff, market held) correct, but range was too tight on the upside.
predictions.confidence_note: ON TRACK as of April 8 close (23,997.35). Thesis: consolidation 23,900-24,100. TCS earnings and April 10 talks are key risks. Confidence 3.

predictions.prediction: MRPL will re-rate toward 200-210 over next 4 weeks as Brent stabilizes near 95-105 post-ceasefire, providing GRM recovery visibility
predictions.reasoning: P002 bearish thesis invalidated by ceasefire. Brent crash from $110 to $93.13 resets crude regime. If Brent stabilizes near $95-105, GRM recovers. Q1FY27 guidance will reflect margin improvement.
predictions.confidence_note: NEW: Entry at ₹182.70 (April 8 close). Brent at $93.13 = GRM recovery thesis active. 30 days to target ₹200-210. Confidence 3.

predictions.prediction: TCS will close above ₹2,600 within 7 days following Q4FY26 results (announced April 9), supported by IT-USD decoupling pattern and stable margin guidance
predictions.reasoning: TCS announcing Q4FY26 results on April 9. IT sector has shown strong momentum (April 7: +2.66%, broad market IT decoupling confirmed). USD strength supports Indian IT export revenue. Ceasefire removes rupee depreciation risk. US Q1 earnings season positive sentiment. Q4 deal wins + revenue guidance will likely provide upside catalyst. TCS at ₹2,555.30 — ₹2,600 target is +1.7% which is modest given earnings beat potential. Multi-factor: IT-USD decoupling (FORECAST_PAT001), Q4 earnings, USD strength, de-escalation regime.
predictions.outcome_evidence: TCS closed at ₹2,542.40 on April 15, below the ₹2,600 target. Despite strong Q4FY26 results (profit +12% YoY, $12B deal wins, ₹31 final dividend), the stock failed to sustain above ₹2,600. Geopolitical macro headwinds (blockade, crude elevated) and broad IT sector pressure weighed. P021 already identified this as WRONG — TCS sell-on-news pattern prevailed. Note: TCS 52-week range ₹2,346-3,630 — stock is at depressed levels, fundamental support at ₹2,420-2,500.
predictions.confidence_note: UPGRADED TO 4★ — TCS Q4FY26 results beat on revenue (₹70,698 cr vs ₹69,932 est), record TCV $12B Q4, ₹31 final dividend. TCS closed ₹2,587.75 on April 9 (before results). Expected gap-up on April 10 to ₹2,620-2,640. P018 target ₹2,600 very likely to be breached on April 10 open. With 6 more days to check_date April 15, probability RIGHT: ~90%+. Confidence raised from 3 to 4★.

predictions.prediction: ADANI PORT will sustain above ₹1,430 and test ₹1,490-1,540 within 7 days as Hormuz safe passage protocol confirms cargo traffic restoration
predictions.reasoning: Hormuz safe passage protocol was confirmed in ceasefire terms on April 8. ADANI PORT benefits directly from shipping traffic restoration through Strait of Hormuz — oil tankers, LNG, cargo. Stock already rallied on April 8 from pre-conflict levels. April 10 Islamabad talks progress = additional catalyst. If talks extend ceasefire to 4+ weeks, port traffic visibility improves significantly. Multi-factor: Hormuz protocol (direct revenue catalyst), rupee stability, PSU infrastructure re-rating, April 10 talks progress.
predictions.outcome_evidence: ADANI PORT closed ₹1,511.80 on April 15, squarely INSIDE the ₹1,490-1,540 target range. Hormuz safe passage protocol thesis played out correctly — port traffic restoration beneficiary. Stock also sustained above ₹1,430 throughout the 7-day window as predicted. April 15 session: ADANI PORT +3.17%, in line with broader PSU infrastructure re-rating.

predictions.prediction: SETFGOLD will correct toward ₹120-122 within 7 days as US-Iran ceasefire progressively compresses gold's geopolitical war premium
predictions.reasoning: SETFGOLD has been the portfolio's war hedge. Gold was at near-ATH on Iranian war premium. April 8 ceasefire + Hormuz safe passage protocol removes the primary geopolitical driver of gold's elevated level. Historical pattern: gold corrects 3-7% within 1 week of confirmed ceasefires in major geopolitical crises. April 10 Islamabad talks may re-introduce some risk premium but overall trajectory is now downward. A005 action exit trigger (ceasefire + Hormuz reopened + gold <$5,100/oz) has now been triggered — this prediction also serves as an A005 exit signal validation. Multi-factor: ceasefire de-escalation, gold war premium compression, risk-on regime (safe-haven demand falling), USD behavior post-ceasefire.
predictions.outcome_evidence: SETFGOLD closed ₹129.31 on April 15, well ABOVE the ₹120-122 target. The thesis was that ceasefire would compress gold's war premium toward ₹120-122. Instead, Islamabad talks failed (April 11), blockade expanded, and Iran IRGC issued 'last warning'. Gold war premium INCREASED, not decreased. SETFGOLD is now ₹129.31 vs entry ₹125.59. The A005 exit signal (gold <$5,100) was NOT triggered — gold is at $4,816 with war premium sustained. Confidence was downgraded to 1★ on April 12 weekly review — prediction correctly pre-identified as WRONG.

predictions.prediction: TCS will test ₹2,650-2,700 by April 16 following strong Q4FY26 beat — record $12B quarterly TCV, revenue beat, AI narrative, ₹31 dividend
predictions.reasoning: TCS Q4FY26 actual results beat analyst estimates on revenue (₹70,698 cr vs ₹69,932 est, +1.1%), announced record quarterly TCV of $12 billion (highest ever), signed 3 mega deals in Q4 (5 in FY26), AI revenue crossed $2.3B annualized, FY26 full-year EBIT margin at 25% (+70bps YoY, highest in 4 years), final dividend ₹31/share (total FY26: ₹110/share). Strong across all key investor metrics. IT sector had already decoupled positively. USD strength tailwind. US earnings season positive context. TCS at ₹2,587.75 — needs +2.4-4.3% to reach target. Confidence 4★ based on multiple positive catalysts converging.
predictions.confidence_note: NEW April 9 EOD. Based on post-market TCS Q4FY26 results beat. P021 check_date April 16 (1 week from results). Risk: April 10 Islamabad talks failure = broad market selloff could cap IT upside temporarily, but TCS-specific catalysts are strong enough to recover within the 7D window.

rca_pattern_log.description: Short-term bullish predictions fail when FII single-day sell > ₹8,000 Cr, regardless of DII support
rca_pattern_log.notes: Apr 6: FII sold ₹8,167 Cr (threshold: >₹8,000 Cr). Second data point supporting PAT001. Pattern is ACTIVE for risk estimates — applying 1.5x multiplier to downside on Apr 7 Nifty. Still need a formal WRONG prediction with PAT001 root cause to promote to framework (requires 2 occurrences). Apr 7 session will be the test — if P003 thesis gets further confirmation today, PAT001 may be promoted in EOD wrap.

last_threshold_event.pattern_note: PAT001 applies more to NEXT DAY reversal risk than same-day — Nifty fell at open Apr 7 confirming delayed effect

rca_pattern_log.root_cause_summary: IndiGo demand resilience vastly underestimated. $100+ Brent causes ATF cost concern but IndiGo has strong pricing power and forward-sold capacity. The 4,100 target required an -8.9% drawdown in 7 days — far too aggressive for a stock with no fundamental deterioration.
rca_pattern_log.missing_signal: T001 (52-week proximity) failed because IndiGo was in an uptrend, not near support. A stock at ₹4,500 needs a catalyst to drop ₹400 in 7 days.
rca_pattern_log.factor_gap: DEMAND_RESILIENCE_FACTOR missing: India aviation demand is inelastic at current ATF levels. Airlines pass costs to consumers. Need quantitative trigger for bearish aviation call.
rca_pattern_log.lesson: For bearish aviation calls: require Brent sustained >$110 for 2+ weeks OR demand shock signal (advance bookings down >20%) OR technical breakdown below key support. Single crude spike insufficient trigger.

rca_pattern_log.root_cause_summary: Defense war premium thesis correct (stock hit ₹1,132, target was 1,050-1,080). BUT intraday reversal on Iran diplomatic outreach (April 14) caused close below target range. FORECAST_PAT003 triggered: Iran outreach = partial de-escalation signal even without formal deal.
rca_pattern_log.missing_signal: DIPLOMATIC_SIGNAL_DEFENSE_OVERRIDE: Any Iran-US outreach (even informal) triggers defense profit-booking. Market frontrunning the potential deal.
rca_pattern_log.factor_gap: No EXIT TRIGGER in defense stock predictions for 'diplomatic signal' scenarios. Need to add partial exit rule.
rca_pattern_log.lesson: For defense stock targets in escalation scenarios: ALWAYS include exit trigger for any diplomatic signal (even informal). At ₹1,100+ profit target: place limit sell at +5% above max target range to auto-lock profits.

rca_pattern_log.description: Defense electronics war-premium persists in extended-truce regimes (2-6 weeks), inverting standard mean-reversion thesis.
rca_pattern_log.notes: April 24: P004 ASTRA MICRO confirmed as 3rd WRONG instance of this pattern (previously P007 SWIGGY-context, P009 ASTRA MICRO). Promotion candidate for next framework revision.

rca_pattern_log.description: When two independent binary events (earnings + geopolitical) have adverse resolution within 5 sessions, Nifty impact is multiplicative not additive.
rca_pattern_log.notes: Apr 24: first observation. P027 NIFTY target 24,600-25,000 breached by -2.86% when IT earnings shock (HCLTECH -10%) + ceasefire-fraying overlapped within 3 sessions.

rca_pattern_log.description: Haven-asset premium persists 2-5 sessions past trigger-event resolution due to positioning unwind lag.
rca_pattern_log.notes: Apr 24: SETFGOLD PARTIAL — closed 0.57% above range ceiling. Haven bid extended through weekend-risk window despite intraday Brent -4.15%.

rca_pattern_log.description: Consumer discretionary names benefit disproportionately from Brent cooling over 2-week windows (real-wage relief signal).
rca_pattern_log.notes: Apr 24: P001 SWIGGY WRONG. Bearish ≤260 thesis broken by Apr 8 ceasefire Brent-crash rally. Consumer disc basket buoyed for the remainder of 4W window.

rca_log.root_cause_summary: Macro regime override — BLOCKADE_ESCALATION_STABILIZED suppressed IT sector despite strong Q4FY26 earnings beat (profit +12%, $12B deals, ₹31 dividend).
rca_log.missing_signal: Sell-on-news pattern in risk-off macro regimes. IT stocks face FII selling + USD/INR uncertainty even when fundamentals beat.
rca_log.factor_gap: No regime filter for IT prediction confidence — predictions should be auto-downgraded to confidence 1-2 during active geopolitical blockades
rca_log.lesson: In BLOCKADE_ESCALATION regimes: IT stocks face triple headwind (risk-off FII, USD/INR uncertainty, macro fear > sector fundamentals). Do not predict IT breakouts during active geopolitical crises regardless of earnings quality.
rca_log.notes: TCS closed ₹2,542 vs ₹2,600 target. Needs 2 more similar observations before M013 promoted to framework.

rca_log.root_cause_summary: Ceasefire fragility underestimated. Islamabad talks failed April 11 (Day 3 of 7-day prediction window), reversing the war premium compression thesis completely.
rca_log.missing_signal: CEASEFIRE_STABILITY_PRECONDITION — bullish ceasefire thesis requires 3+ day stability before acting on war premium compression. Used single-point ceasefire announcement.
rca_log.factor_gap: No "ceasefire hold probability" decay model. April 8 ceasefire had <3 day stability before talks collapsed.
rca_log.lesson: Before making bearish gold/safe-haven predictions on ceasefire news: (1) Wait 3+ trading days to confirm ceasefire holds. (2) Model at 60%+ probability that ceasefire is temporary. (3) Only reduce gold hedge once formal written agreement signed, not verbal announcement.
rca_log.notes: This is the 2nd occurrence of ceasefire fragility causing a wrong call (April 8-11 cycle). FORECAST_PAT004 promoted to framework.

rca_log.root_cause_summary: N/A — RIGHT prediction. Target range (₹4,150-4,300) was too conservative but directional call correct.
rca_log.missing_signal: ATF relief rally magnitude underestimated. INDIGO hit ₹4,638 vs ₹4,300 range ceiling.
rca_log.factor_gap: Aviation stocks benefit non-linearly from Brent decline: every $10 Brent decline = ~₹200-300 INDIGO appreciation given operational leverage
rca_log.lesson: For aviation stocks: Brent sensitivity is non-linear. At $10 Brent decline from peak ($102→$92), INDIGO can see 8-10% rally from ATF cost relief + sentiment. Set aviation targets at least 2× the initial estimate when Brent decline thesis is active.
rca_log.notes: Scored RIGHT but lesson noted for target calibration

rca_log.root_cause_summary: MACRO_REGIME_OVERRIDE_FUNDAMENTALS — 2nd confirmation. Strong Q4FY26 beat (record $12B TCV, revenue ₹70,698 Cr) could not overcome BLOCKADE_ESCALATION regime. Sell-on-news pattern persisted for full 7-day window. TCS closed ₹2,576.90 vs ₹2,650-2,700 target.
rca_log.missing_signal: M013 (IT Regime Risk Filter) — now 2 occurrences (P018, P021). IT stocks need regime change before fundamentals-driven breakout calls succeed.
rca_log.factor_gap: No automatic confidence downgrade for IT predictions during active geopolitical blockades. Framework should auto-cap IT prediction confidence at 2★ during BLOCKADE_ESCALATION.
rca_log.lesson: 2nd occurrence of IT WRONG during blockade. M013 pattern now at 2 confirmations — candidate for framework promotion. Auto-cap IT predictions at confidence 2★ in BLOCKADE_ESCALATION regime.
rca_log.notes: M013 at 2 occurrences (P018 + P021). Needs 1 more for framework promotion OR can be promoted at next weekly review given strong signal.

rca_log.root_cause_summary: DIPLOMATIC_RESTART_NOT_ANTICIPATED — bearish Nifty prediction (23,200-23,700) made on April 12 after Islamabad talks failure. Within 2 days, Trump signaled deal optimism and diplomatic restart. Nifty closed 24,196 on April 16 — 500+ pts above target range upper bound.
rca_log.missing_signal: No auto-invalidation clause for diplomatic restart signals. When Trump reverses stance on negotiations, bearish thesis can collapse within 1-2 sessions.
rca_log.factor_gap: Framework lacks DIPLOMATIC_RESTART_TRIGGER — need pattern that monitors Trump statements for deal optimism signals and auto-invalidates bearish predictions.
rca_log.lesson: Bearish predictions after talk failures should have 48-hour auto-review trigger. If Trump signals willingness to resume talks within 48 hours of failure, immediately invalidate bearish thesis.
rca_log.notes: 1st occurrence. Watching for 2nd to promote. Related to FORECAST_PAT003 (binary underweighting) — diplomatic restart is a variant of "best case scenario materializing faster than expected."

rca_log.root_cause_summary: MACRO_REGIME_OVERRIDE_PERSISTENT — 3rd consecutive TCS post-earnings reversion prediction WRONG (P018, P021, P022). Even on a +0.65% Nifty rally day with Brent collapsing -3.19% and ceasefire progress, TCS moved only +0.18%. IT sector remained regime-trapped: WIPRO -2.83% (guidance hangover), INFY -0.04%. Target >₹2,600 missed by ₹18.50 (0.71%).
rca_log.missing_signal: IT sector regime override was under-weighted. Sell-on-news pattern + IT cyclical weakness + guidance concerns combined to suppress any reversion through the full 7D window.
rca_log.factor_gap: M013 (IT Regime Risk Filter) now at 3 occurrences — PROMOTE to validated framework factor. Rule: during IT sector regime weakness (defined as sector down 3%+ in rolling 5D AND heavyweight guidance concerns), do NOT forecast post-earnings reversion on IT heavyweights for horizons <10D.
rca_log.lesson: IT sell-on-news reversion predictions are structurally unfit when the IT regime itself is bearish. Stop generating short-horizon IT reversion calls until sector regime resets (e.g., a 3D+ sector rally with positive guidance catalyst). Keep TCS watchlist but no new 7D calls until sector re-opens.
rca_log.notes: 3rd occurrence — promoting from pattern to validated factor. Aligns with L1 IT sector accuracy 0/2 (0%).

rca_log.root_cause_summary: DIPLOMATIC_MOMENTUM_OVERRODE_WAR_PREMIUM — Prediction made on April 12 after Islamabad talks failure assumed sustained gold bid through April 22 deadline. By April 14-17 Trump signaled deal optimism, US-Iran second round talks announced, ceasefire extension "in principle agreement" reported, Brent collapsed -6% from $102 peak to $96.22. Gold bid faded: SETFGOLD ₹128.69 vs ₹130-134 target (short ₹1.31, 1.0%).
rca_log.missing_signal: No auto-invalidation clause for diplomatic restart signals after talks failures. Gold safe-haven thesis collapsed within 48h of Trump reversing stance on Iran talks. Same pattern as P024 (NIFTY bearish after talks failure) and P020 (SETFGOLD earlier WRONG).
rca_log.factor_gap: FORECAST_PAT005 (Diplomatic Restart Trigger) now at 2nd occurrence — PROMOTE to validated framework. Rule: any bearish/safe-haven thesis predicated on a talks failure should auto-invalidate within 48h if the same principal (Trump/VP) reverses stance or announces new talks round.
rca_log.lesson: When a geopolitical crisis has a "talks failed" event, ANY safe-haven/bearish call with horizon >48h must assume 30%+ probability of diplomatic reversal. For gold specifically, when Brent falls >5% from crisis peak AND new talks round announced, close gold bullish thesis immediately.
rca_log.notes: 2nd occurrence of FORECAST_PAT005 (1st was RCA_P024 NIFTY April 16). PROMOTING to validated framework pattern.

rca_log.root_cause_summary: PSU infrastructure stock DII support overrode mean-reversion thesis. IRFC stayed above ₹103-106 throughout the 10D horizon despite RSI overbought. No negative PSU catalyst materialized.
rca_log.missing_signal: PSU_DII_SUPPORT_FILTER — DII flows into PSU names create persistent price support that overrides technical mean-reversion signals
rca_log.factor_gap: No DII flow durability check in framework for PSU calls
rca_log.lesson: For government PSU stocks, RSI overbought alone is insufficient for mean-reversion call. Require a specific negative catalyst: capex cut, FY guidance miss, credit event, or sector derating. Count: 1 occurrence (P026). 1 more needed for promotion.

rca_log.root_cause_summary: Bearish thesis ≤₹260 invalidated by April 8 ceasefire rally which lifted consumer discretionary. Subsequent Apr 22-24 re-escalation did not carry SWIGGY back below ₹280 because Q4 results window neutralized sector overhang. Stock closed ₹287.10, 10.4% above target ceiling.
rca_log.missing_signal: CONSUMER_DISC_BRENT_INVERSE — consumer discretionary benefits disproportionately from Brent cooling; the same mechanism that hurts IT helps SWIGGY.
rca_log.factor_gap: S004 consumer discretionary structural bid not weighted against Brent inverse correlation.
rca_log.lesson: Bearish calls on consumer discretionary during elevated-Brent regimes must price in Brent-cooling optionality. 4-week horizon is long enough to capture a ceasefire regime flip.

rca_log.root_cause_summary: Defense premium unwind thesis (target 850-900) failed. Stock rallied to ~₹1,130 on extended-truce war-premium regime and held through the ceasefire-fraying wave. 3rd observation of M012_DEFENSE_SECTOR_DURATION_PREMIUM pattern (P007, P009, P004) — candidate for factor promotion.
rca_log.missing_signal: M012_DEFENSE_SECTOR_DURATION_PREMIUM — defense electronics thrive on EXTENDED tension regimes (2-6 weeks), not binary resolution.
rca_log.factor_gap: High-PE concern (50.6x) was used as a bear thesis without sector rotation durability check.
rca_log.lesson: 3 occurrences now observed (P007, P009, P004). Ready for formal framework promotion to active factor. Defense pullback calls need a specific trigger: sector PE contraction, budget disappointment, or ceasefire >4 weeks.

rca_log.root_cause_summary: Nifty target 24,600-25,000 breached by -2.86%. Three bearish sessions Apr 22-24 (cumulative -2.78%) driven by: (1) ceasefire-fraying (Iran refused talks, Israel threats), (2) IT sector cascade (HCLTECH -10% Apr 22, Infosys -6.93% Apr 24), (3) FII 4-day selling.
rca_log.missing_signal: MULTI_BINARY_CORRELATION — when two independent binary events (earnings + geopolitical) have adverse resolution within 5 sessions, Nifty impact is multiplicative, not additive.
rca_log.factor_gap: M013 (IT Regime Risk Filter) + FORECAST_PAT003 (Geopolitical Binary Underweighting) were not fully promoted when target was set.
rca_log.lesson: Nifty targets need scenario-matrix with multi-binary cases. Single-point range forecasts fail when two adverse binaries resolve within one week.

rca_log.root_cause_summary: SETFGOLD ₹127.73 closed 0.57% above range ceiling (127). Gold haven bid persisted fractionally longer than tactical forecast due to Apr 23-24 Israel-Iran escalation. Intraday touched ₹128.87 Apr 23 then cooled with Brent -4.15% Apr 24. Range near-miss.
rca_log.missing_signal: HAVEN_PREMIUM_DURATION_TAIL — haven bids persist 2-5 sessions past trigger-event resolution due to positioning unwind lag.
rca_log.factor_gap: Gold ETF dual-driver (USD-gold + geopolitical bid) stickier than modeled tactical-horizon unwind.
rca_log.lesson: Haven-asset range calls should extend the upper bound by 0.5-1% when a geopolitical binary is still active during the forecast window. Partial grading appropriate when within 1% of range.

rca_log.root_cause_summary: Forecast built on Brent $111.16 EXTREME_ELEVATED regime and feared Bajaj Finance provisions surge. Neither adverse catalyst materialized: (1) Bajaj Finance PAT +22-23% YoY — no provisions surge. (2) UAE OPEC exit (effective May 1) sent structural supply-increase signal, capping Brent upside. RELIEF_RALLY scenario (15% prob, range 24,000-24,200) materialized via earnings surprise mechanism rather than Hormuz reopening as scripted. Close 24,177 was within RELIEF_RALLY range.
rca_log.missing_signal: EARNINGS_SURPRISE_OVERRIDES_MACRO_FEAR: Strong large-cap financial result (Bajaj Finance) can trigger sector-wide recovery even in EXTREME_ELEVATED crude regime. Pattern candidate.
rca_log.factor_gap: Bajaj Finance provisions risk was 'feared but not quantified' — P010-level concern without systematic estimate of upside scenario probability.

proposed_pattern.description: When a major feared earnings catalyst (provisions, NIM collapse, guidance shock) does NOT materialize, the reversal can be 1.0-1.5% vs the macro headwind expectation.

rca_log.root_cause_summary: P044 was designed as a replacement for P017 (which targeted 200-210 and was failing). Both shared a BULLISH bias on MRPL via OMC GRM relief thesis tied to crude normalization. The May 7 Brent low ($99.10) seemed to validate the thesis briefly, but the May 11 re-escalation (Trump rejects Iran proposal, Brent +6% to $105.70) collapsed the entire OMC GRM relief narrative. MRPL has high crude-sensitivity asymmetry — it benefits modestly when Brent < $100 but gets hit hard above $103.
rca_log.missing_signal: OMC_GRM_RELIEF_REGIME_REQUIRES_BRENT_SUSTAINED_SUB_100: Single sub-$100 print (May 7) is insufficient — need 3 consecutive sessions sub-$100 to validate regime change. The OMC trade is essentially binary on the $100 line.
rca_log.factor_gap: M013/M016 (Brent regime sensitivity for OMC) was acknowledged but the probability-weighted impact of a single weekend re-escalation event was under-weighted. Confidence 2 was correct but range-low (162) was too aggressive against support at 158-160.
rca_log.lesson: For range-bound forecasts on commodity-sensitive PSUs, set range_low at least 2-3% below current price to avoid being whipsawed by 1-day regime flips. Use crude-regime gating: do not initiate OMC bullish prediction unless Brent has been sub-$100 for 3+ sessions.

rca_log.actual: BankNifty 54,439.90 on May 11 (BELOW range_low 55,000 by 1.02% = 560 pts)
rca_log.root_cause_summary: FORECAST_PAT005 (Diplomatic Restart Trigger) was the central engine of P045 — it assumed Iran peace process continuity would unwind credit-rotation fears and produce BankNifty short-covering. The pattern fired correctly on May 1-2 (Brent fell to $99 area, S&P ATH), but the WEEKEND_REVERSAL (Trump rejects Iran response May 10-11) collapsed the underlying premise. FII sold for the 5th consecutive week through May 8, then Monday May 11 brought a -1.57% BankNifty session breaking the 55,000 floor entirely.
rca_log.missing_signal: FORECAST_PAT005_FRAGILITY: Diplomatic Restart Trigger is contingent on continued de-escalation signal — a single weekend reversal can invalidate the entire pattern. Need a fragility filter: if peace process is < 7 sessions old AND no formal MoU/treaty signed, downgrade pattern-derived predictions by one confidence star.
rca_log.factor_gap: Geopolitical regime durability not adequately modeled. Pattern-based predictions need an "regime decay rate" parameter when the underlying signal is < 2 weeks old.
rca_log.lesson: BankNifty predictions in a fragile-ceasefire regime should not assume floor levels hold absent a structural support (e.g., RBI policy event, durable FII reversal). 55,000 was treated as floor; it was actually a level being defended by DII alone against systematic FII selling. Reset BankNifty floor to 54,000-54,200 in current regime.

rca_log.forecast: BankNifty will fail to close above 57,000 by May 5
rca_log.actual: BankNifty did not close above 57,000 in the May 1-5 window (CORRECT)
rca_log.note: Retro-scored on May 11 due to EOD scoring gap May 8-10. Negative-framed predictions (X will fail to do Y) tend to score well in choppy regimes — should be deployed more often as a bias-counterweight tool.

rca_log.root_cause_summary: Direction was correctly bearish but the BEARISH_RE_ESCALATION scenario (30% probability) was the actual outcome — and even that scenario's range (23,950-24,100) was overshot to the downside. The compound shock was: (1) Trump weekend rejection of Iran proposal, (2) Brent jumping 4-6% to $105.70, (3) PM Modi austerity message read as a domestic growth warning. Item (3) was not anticipated and acted as an India-specific amplifier on top of the global re-escalation move.
rca_log.missing_signal: POLITICAL_AUSTERITY_SIGNAL_AS_GROWTH_WARNING: A national-leader austerity message during a geopolitical stress episode acts as a confirmation signal of expected economic damage, deepening selloffs. Pattern candidate.
rca_log.factor_gap: Weekend tail-risk weighting in re-escalation regimes: scenario probabilities should re-weight Friday-to-Monday as +10-15% to the bearish tail when a binary geopolitical event is unresolved over the weekend.

proposed_pattern.description: When a sitting national leader issues an austerity/conservation message during a geopolitical stress episode (especially crude shock for an importer), expect 30-50% additional downside vs base-case selloff because the message is interpreted as official forecast of economic damage.

rca_log.lesson_for_next_forecast: In re-escalation regimes with Brent +4%/day moves, calibrate session_direction.change_pct_estimate to -0.8 to -1.5% (not -0.2%). Use range_low 1.5-2% below open.

rca_log.rca_summary: Five predictions scored WRONG/ABOVE_RANGE on the May 12 wrap. Common root cause: FORECAST_PAT005 (Diplomatic Restart Trigger) reversed across the May 10-11 weekend when Trump rejected the Iran counter-offer. P041 and P043 were directly anchored on pattern continuity; P017 was the legacy MRPL re-rate thesis (Brent stabilization didn't hold); P039 was an IT underperformance call that needed an in-window catalyst that slipped; P003 was a legacy thesis already invalidated by April gap. New pattern candidate: WEEKEND_REGIME_REVERSAL_PUNCTURES_THESIS.

patterns_proposed.name: WEEKEND_REGIME_REVERSAL_PUNCTURES_THESIS
patterns_proposed.description: When a thesis depends on continuation of a recent (≤2-week-old) pattern and a 2-day market gap (weekend) coincides with a binary policy decision (e.g., peace counter-offer accepted/rejected), reversal probability ≥ 50%. All pattern-anchored bullish predictions inside that window should auto-downgrade confidence by 1 star.

patterns_proposed.name: CATALYST_TIMING_DEPENDENCY
patterns_proposed.description: Pattern-based predictions (e.g., SELL_ON_EARNINGS_NEWS_IT) without a confirmed in-window catalyst date expire uneventfully ~70% of the time. Require a confirmed earnings/event date inside the prediction window before assigning ≥3-star confidence.

patterns_proposed.name: PEACE_DEAL_REVERSAL_RE_ANCHORS_HAVEN
patterns_proposed.description: Gold/silver haven premium does not unwind when a peace deal is REVERSED mid-process — it re-anchors at the elevated pre-rally level. Use ABOVE_RANGE rather than WRONG when haven correction predictions miss by <1.5% in this scenario.

scored_predictions.note: Defense run accelerated beyond target on ceasefire collapse. Non-linear regime breakout.
PSU mean-reversion thesis failed. DII support + government capex narrative sustained elevated price.
Consumer disc Brent-inverse override. Ceasefire rally kept stock above 280.
Defense duration premium — 3rd occurrence. M012 promotion candidate.
Multi-binary (IT cascade + ceasefire fraying) dragged Nifty -2.86% below target.
Range near-miss 0.57% above ceiling. Haven bid duration tail.

scored_predictions.target: HCLTECH fails to sustain above ₹1,430 post Q4FY26 (M013 sell-on-news pattern)
scored_predictions.evidence: HCLTECH closed ₹1,196 on April 28 — ₹234 below ₹1,430 ceiling. SELL_ON_EARNINGS_NEWS_IT 5th confirmation.
scored_predictions.lesson: M013 IT Regime Risk Filter performing as expected. 5 confirmations of post-earnings IT weakness in active geopolitical regime.

scored_predictions.evidence: IRFC at ₹105.14 on April 29 — 10.7% above range ceiling ₹95. DII infrastructure buying support intact throughout period.
scored_predictions.root_cause: DII systematic infrastructure mandates create consistent support floor well above fundamental fair-value for PSU rail-finance names. Second consecutive IRFC bearish call failure (P026 was also WRONG).
scored_predictions.lesson: PSU_DII_FILTER: Avoid bearish PSU infra calls when DII systematic buying is active. IRFC 2x confirmed as DII-supported. Do not make bearish IRFC/IRCON range calls in current regime.

scored_predictions.evidence: ASTRA MICRO closed ₹1,104.10 on May 5. 5.1% above threshold. M012 v5 defense floor held: Brent $112.78 (>> $92 threshold), Hormuz actively contested. Defense premium structural in Iran escalation regime.
scored_predictions.rca: CORRECT — M012 v5 defense floor thesis validated. Prediction correctly assessed that defense premium unwind conditions (Brent <$92 + Hormuz open 5D) would NOT be met in the 10-day window. Consistent with broader defense sector pattern (MAZDOCK, COCHINSHIP also sustained).

scored_predictions.evidence: BankNifty closed 54,547.05. Never approached 57,000 — 4.5% below threshold. FII selling 10th consecutive week. ECL structural headwind confirmed.
scored_predictions.rca: CORRECT — BankNifty structural headwind thesis. FII selling persistence and credit rotation risk in geopolitical regime materialized. Note: BankNifty actually underperformed forecast range (stayed sub-55,000 vs 54,500-56,500 expected). ECL headwind more severe than estimated.

scored_predictions.evidence: Nifty IT UNDERPERFORMED Nifty. Nifty +~9.4% (21,964→24,033 from Apr 7). IT stocks flat-to-negative on May 5 (TCS -0.16%, HCLTECH -0.02%, WIPRO -0.48%, TECHM -1.32%). M013 active throughout (Brent >$100). SELL_ON_EARNINGS_NEWS_IT fired 5+ times.
scored_predictions.rca: ROOT CAUSE: M013 (IT underperforms in Brent >$100 + Iran escalation regime) is a dominant override factor that was NOT adequately weighted at prediction time. USD strength thesis was valid in isolation but lost to geopolitical risk-off rotation. SELL_ON_EARNINGS_NEWS_IT pattern (HCLTECH, WIPRO post-results) compounded the headwind. Confidence was correctly downgraded to 1★ on Apr 26 audit — but prediction should have been INVALIDATED (not just downgraded) given M013 + IT_CASCADE active. LESSON: When M013 active, no IT outperformance prediction should be initiated/maintained. P039 correctly superseded this prediction.

scored_predictions.evidence: MRPL closed ₹160.33 on May 11 (after target May 8). Below range_low by 1.67. OMC GRM relief thesis collapsed on Brent +6% rebound to $105.70.
BankNifty closed 54,439.90 on May 11 = 560 pts below range_low 55,000. FORECAST_PAT005 short-covering trigger collapsed when Iran-US standoff re-escalated over weekend.
BankNifty did not close above 57,000 in the May 1-5 window. FII systematic selling + credit-rotation thesis validated.

scored_predictions.note: Target 22,000-22,200 never reached. Thesis invalidated since Apr 8 gap.
Target 200-210 never approached. Locked WRONG in May 7 EOD.
3-5% underperformance vs Nifty 50 did not materialize Apr 28 - May 8 window.
Nifty closed 23,379.55 vs target 24,300-24,600. 920 pts below range_low. FORECAST_PAT005 reversal.
SETFGOLD 128.29 — 1.29 above range_high 127. Peace deal reversal re-anchored haven premium.
